mechanism: Inhibition of BCL-2 proteins leading to apoptosis of leukemic cells
additional_notes: Initially used for chronic lymphocytic leukemia (CLL)

mechanism: Targets SRC family kinases and BCR-ABL
additional_notes: Used for chronic myeloid leukemia

mechanism: Blocks BCR-ABL kinase activity, inhibiting leukemia cell proliferation
additional_notes: Used in chronic myeloid leukemia (CML)

mechanism: Inhibits RAF kinase and several receptor tyrosine kinases
additional_notes: Used in various cancers including liver and renal cancer

mechanism: Inhibits JAK1/2 signaling, reducing cytokine signaling and proliferation
additional_notes: Used for myelofibrosis and polycythemia vera

mechanism: Inhibits topoisomerase II, leading to DNA damage and apoptosis
additional_notes: Used in various cancers including small cell lung cancer

mechanism: Induces cell cycle arrest and apoptosis by increasing acetylation of histones
additional_notes: Also known as SAHA, used in cutaneous T-cell lymphoma

mechanism: Induces differentiation and apoptosis of leukemia cells
additional_notes: Used in acute promyelocytic leukemia

mechanism: Inhibits 26S proteasome activity, promoting apoptosis of myeloma cells
additional_notes: Used for multiple myeloma

mechanism: Competes with estrogen for binding estrogen receptors, inhibiting transcription of estrogen-responsive genes
additional_notes: Used mainly for breast cancer treatment

mechanism: Interferes with lysosomal degradation and autophagy
additional_notes: Shows anticancer potential beyond malaria treatment

mechanism: Lowers hepatic glucose production and has an emerging role in cancer metabolism alteration
additional_notes: Commonly used for type 2 diabetes

mechanism: Blocks COX-2 enzyme involved in inflammation and apoptosis
additional_notes: Used for arthritis pain and inflammation

mechanism: Inhibits the hedgehog signaling and abnormal angiogenesis
additional_notes: Shows potential as an anticancer agent beyond antifungal use

mechanism: Inhibits bacterial DNA-dependent RNA synthesis; potential antiproliferative effects
additional_notes: Primarily used for tuberculosis

mechanism: Decreases cholesterol production, with emerging cancer therapy potential
additional_notes: Typically used as a cholesterol-lowering medication

mechanism: Inhibits lysosomal function and autophagy similar to chloroquine
additional_notes: Shows promise in cancer therapy

mechanism: Inhibits angiogenesis and modulates immune response
additional_notes: Used in multiple myeloma and has a controversial history

mechanism: Activates the NRF2 pathway, leading to enhanced cellular defense
additional_notes: Used for multiple sclerosis

mechanism: Inhibits acetaldehyde dehydrogenase, with emerging anticancer properties
additional_notes: Previously used for alcohol dependency treatment
